paper_info.title: PAPER2CODE: AUTOMATING CODE GENERATION FROM SCIENTIFIC PAPERS IN MACHINE LEARNING

Perception_ContextAcquisition: The goal of our repository-level code generation task is to automatically produce a repository that faithfully implements methods and experiments described in machine learning papers.
Environment_Interaction: The model then identifies components (to be implemented), categorizes them into three severity levels (high, medium, and low), and critiques how well each component is implemented.
Tool_CapabilityUse: PaperCoder operates in three stages: planning, where it constructs a high-level roadmap, designs the system architecture with diagrams, identifies file dependencies, and generates configuration files; analysis, which focuses on interpreting implementation-specific details; and generation, where modular, dependency-aware code is produced.
Memory_StateManagement: The analysis phase focuses on interpreting and specifying the implementation-level details for modules within each file.
Reasoning_Planning: Each phase is instantiated through a set of specialized agents designed to collaborate effectively across the pipeline.
Action_Execution: The final stage is the coding phase, where the complete code repository is produced.
Feedback_Reflection: We perform model-based evaluation with GPT-4o on all the collected repositories and select the top 30 from each venue.
Human_Collaboration: We also perform human evaluations with the authors of the original papers, to ensure reliable judgments and to assess the quality of our model-based evaluations by measuring their correlation with human scores.
Role_MultiagentCoordination: Moreover, each phase is instantiated through a set of specialized agents designed to collaborate effectively across the pipeline.
Governance_Traceability: We ensure the quality and reliability of human evaluations by measuring the inter-annotator agreement based on Cohen’s kappa coefficient, which exhibits a high score of 0.79, indicating strong consistency.

RQ3.task_context: transforming machine learning papers into operational code repositories
